---
title: "Rotation As Addition"
---

A rotation `rot(n,k)` moves a node by adding `k` in `C_n`.

<div class="dictionary-box" data-dictionary-id="CC-0101"></div>

<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="CC-T0001"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="CC-T0002"></div>
<div class="theorem-box" data-theorem-id="CC-T0003"></div>

<div class="widget-panel" data-widget="rotation_composition" data-theorem-id="CC-T0002" data-dictionary-ids="CC-0101"></div>

## Source Links

<p data-paper-ref="papers/PAPER_01_FINITE_CIRCLES.md">Paper source: [Circle Calculus I](../../../papers/PAPER_01_FINITE_CIRCLES.md)</p>

Python reference model:

```python
from circle_math.finite import Circle

C = Circle(12)
C.rot(C.rot(8, 5), 7) == C.rot(8, 12)
```

<script type="module" src="../../widgets/shared/widget_base.js"></script>
<script type="module" src="../../widgets/S1/rotation_composition.js"></script>
